## claude-in-chrome
**IMPORTANT: Before using any chrome browser tools, you MUST first load them using ToolSearch.**

Chrome browser tools are MCP tools that require loading before use. Before calling any mcp__claude-in-chrome__* tool:
1. Use ToolSearch with `select:mcp__claude-in-chrome__<tool_name>` to load the specific tool
2. Then call the tool

For example, to get tab context:
1. First: ToolSearch with query "select:mcp__claude-in-chrome__tabs_context_mcp"
2. Then: Call mcp__claude-in-chrome__tabs_context_mcp

## context7
Use this server to fetch current documentation whenever the user asks about a library, framework, SDK, API, CLI tool, or cloud service -- even well-known ones like React, Next.js, Prisma, Express, Tailwind, Django, or Spring Boot. This includes API syntax, configuration, version migration, library-specific debugging, setup instructions, and CLI tool usage. Use even when you think you know the answer -- your training data may not reflect recent changes. Prefer this over web search for library docs.

Do not use for: refactoring, writing scripts from scratch, debugging business logic, code review, or general programming concepts.

- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.

- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.

Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) on a cron schedule or once at a specific time. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring or one-time remote agent ("run this every Monday", "open a cleanup PR for X in 2 weeks"), or to manage existing routines. ALSO OFFER PROACTIVELY: after you finish work that has a natural future follow-up, end your reply with a one-line offer to schedule a background agent to do it. Strong signals: a feature flag / gate / experiment / staged rollout was just shipped (offer a one-time agent in ~2 weeks to open a cleanup PR or evaluate results), a new alert/monitor was created (offer a recurring agent to triage it), a TODO/migration with a "remove once X" condition was left behind (offer a one-time agent to do the removal). Name a concrete action and cadence ("in 2 weeks", "every Monday") and only offer when the run just succeeded — do not pitch a schedule for something that has not happened yet.

1	# Plan : crash bundle.module + onboarding sans segments par défaut
2	
3	## Contexte
4	
5	Deux problèmes liés à la première utilisation après installation Homebrew :
6	
7	1. **Crash au démarrage** : un utilisateur installant l'app via Homebrew (cask `v0.1.0`) obtient un `EXC_BREAKPOINT` sur le main thread. La stack montre :
8	   ```
9	   _assertionFailure
10	   closure #1 in variable initialization expression of static NSBundle.module
11	   VendorBranding.loadTemplateImage(named:)
12	   VendorIconView.body.getter
13	   ```
14	   `Bundle.module` (synthétisé par SwiftPM) trap quand le resource bundle `AIUsagesTrackers_AIUsagesTrackers.bundle` est introuvable à côté de l'exécutable. Le commit `5c70139` a ajouté la copie des bundles dans `Contents/MacOS/` du `.app`, mais **ce fix n'est pas dans la release `v0.1.0`** (le cask Homebrew télécharge un zip d'avant le fix). De plus, `Bundle.module` qui trap est fragile : n'importe quelle régression de packaging recasse l'app sans recovery possible.
15	
16	2. **Onboarding** : 2 segments sont seedés par défaut au premier lancement (`MenuBarSegmentsSeeder.defaultSegments()`), ce qui suppose que l'utilisateur utilise déjà Claude. Il faut au contraire l'inviter à configurer son premier segment via un CTA explicite dans la barre de menu (« Configure AI Metrics »).
17	
18	Objectifs : rendre l'app non-crashable même si un asset manque, et faire en sorte que la première ouverture invite à la configuration plutôt que de pré-supposer l'usage.
19	
20	---
21	
22	## Approche
23	
24	### Problème 1 — Résolution défensive de Bundle.module
25	
26	Remplacer l'usage direct de `Bundle.module` (qui `fatalError` si introuvable) par un helper qui :
27	- Tente la résolution standard, mais dans une fonction qui ne propage pas le trap (le trap est une `precondition`, on ne peut pas le `try` ; il faut éviter d'y arriver).
28	- Cherche le `.bundle` à plusieurs emplacements connus :
29	  1. `Bundle.main.url(forResource:..., withExtension:..., subdirectory: "VendorBranding")` — au cas où les ressources seraient à la racine de `Contents/Resources/`.
30	  2. `Bundle(url: Bundle.main.bundleURL.appending(...))` pour `Contents/MacOS/AIUsagesTrackers_AIUsagesTrackers.bundle` (emplacement utilisé par le script de build) puis `Contents/Resources/AIUsagesTrackers_AIUsagesTrackers.bundle`.
31	  3. En dernier recours, tenter `Bundle.module` *uniquement* via une référence indirecte protégée (en pratique, si on a déjà tout cherché manuellement, on n'y va pas).
32	- Retourne `nil` proprement si rien n'est trouvé, faisant que `VendorIconView` affiche son fallback (la lettre du vendor) au lieu de crasher l'app entière.
33	
34	### Problème 2 — Plus de segments par défaut + CTA menu bar
35	
36	- `MenuBarSegmentsSeeder.defaultSegments()` retourne `[]`. Le seeder reste appelé pour positionner `menuBarSegmentsInitialized = true` (idempotence préservée).
37	- Dans `MenuBarLabelRenderer.render(...)`, quand `segments.isEmpty`, utiliser le texte « Configure AI Metrics » (avec une icône `gearshape` ou similaire en préfixe pour le rendre repérable) au lieu de `"--"`.
38	- Le popover existant (`UsageDetailsView`) s'ouvre déjà au clic via `togglePopover`. Vérifier qu'il a un état vide cohérent qui guide vers Settings ; sinon ajouter un bouton « Open Settings » bien en évidence.
39	- Une fois qu'au moins un segment est configuré, le rendu reprend la logique actuelle.
40	
41	### Release
42	
43	Une fois les deux fixes mergés, taguer `v0.1.1` et laisser la CI release publier le nouveau cask. Cela permet aux utilisateurs Homebrew existants de faire `brew upgrade` et d'obtenir un binaire qui ne crashe plus, même si pour une raison X le bundle ressources manque.
44	
45	---
46	
47	## Fichiers à modifier
48	
49	| Fichier | Lignes | Modification |
50	|---|---|---|
51	| `AIUsagesTrackers/Sources/App/VendorBranding.swift` | 105-116 | Remplacer `Bundle.module.url(...)` par un lookup multi-emplacements avec retour `nil` safe. Ne plus référencer `Bundle.module` directement. |
52	| `AIUsagesTrackers/Sources/AIUsagesTrackers/Preferences/MenuBarSegmentsSeeder.swift` | 9-24 | `defaultSegments()` retourne `[]`. |
53	| `AIUsagesTrackers/Sources/App/MenuBarLabelRenderer.swift` | 17-40 (et helpers) | Quand `segments.isEmpty` ET `fallbackText` indique l'absence de données utiles, rendre le label « Configure AI Metrics ». Préférable : ajouter un paramètre explicite `isUnconfigured: Bool` calculé dans `AppDelegate.refreshStatusItemImage()` à partir de `store.menuBarSegments.isEmpty`. |
54	| `AIUsagesTrackers/Sources/App/AppDelegate.swift` | 173-183 | Passer le flag `isUnconfigured` au renderer. |
55	| `AIUsagesTrackers/Sources/App/Views/UsageDetailsView.swift` (à confirmer) | empty state | S'assurer que l'utilisateur sans segment voit dans le popover un CTA explicite « Open Settings to add your first segment ». |
56	| `AIUsagesTrackers/Tests/...MenuBarSegmentsSeederTests.swift` | – | Mettre à jour le test qui asserte `defaultSegments().count == 2` (s'il existe) pour refléter le nouveau comportement (`isEmpty`). |
57	| `AIUsagesTrackers/Tests/...VendorBrandingTests.swift` (à créer si absent) | – | Test : `loadTemplateImage` retourne `nil` proprement quand le bundle de resources est absent (au lieu de crasher). |
58	
59	## Réutilisation existante
60	
61	- Le state vide UI dans Settings (`MenubarHintSettingsView.swift:115-133`) est déjà bien fait — on peut s'en inspirer pour l'état vide du popover s'il manque.
62	- Le script `scripts/build-app-bundle.sh:49-83` copie déjà les `.bundle` dans `Contents/MacOS/` ; le fix défensif est en plus, pas en remplacement.
63	- L'observabilité dark/light mode (`refreshStatusItemImage`) reste inchangée.
64	
65	## Vérification
66	
67	1. **Build local** : `swift build` puis `./scripts/build-app-bundle.sh` → lancer le `.app` produit.
68	2. **Test crash defensiveness** : supprimer manuellement `Contents/MacOS/AIUsagesTrackers_AIUsagesTrackers.bundle` du `.app` et relancer → l'app doit démarrer, la barre de menu doit afficher « Configure AI Metrics » avec un fallback texte pour les icônes vendor (pas de crash).
69	3. **Test onboarding** : effacer les UserDefaults (`defaults delete io.github.fcamblor.ai-usages-tracker`), relancer → la barre de menu affiche « Configure AI Metrics », aucun segment n'est seedé. Cliquer ouvre le popover ou les settings, ajouter un segment → le label se met à jour.
70	4. **Tests Swift** : `swift test` doit passer (tests seeder + nouveau test VendorBranding).
71	5. **CI release** : tag `v0.1.1` → vérifier que le nouveau zip publie bien le `.app` avec `Contents/MacOS/*.bundle` présent.
72

Remplacer l'usage direct de `Bundle.module` (qui `fatalError` si introuvable) par un helper qui :
- Tente la résolution standard, mais dans une fonction qui ne propage pas le trap (le trap est une `precondition`, on ne peut pas le `try` ; il faut éviter d'y arriver).
- Cherche le `.bundle` à plusieurs emplacements connus :
  1. `Bundle.main.url(forResource:..., withExtension:..., subdirectory: "VendorBranding")` — au cas où les ressources seraient à la racine de `Contents/Resources/`.
  2. `Bundle(url: Bundle.main.bundleURL.appending(...))` pour `Contents/MacOS/AIUsagesTrackers_AIUsagesTrackers.bundle` (emplacement utilisé par le script de build) puis `Contents/Resources/AIUsagesTrackers_AIUsagesTrackers.bundle`.
  3. En dernier recours, tenter `Bundle.module` *uniquement* via une référence indirecte protégée (en pratique, si on a déjà tout cherché manuellement, on n'y va pas).
- Retourne `nil` proprement si rien n'est trouvé, faisant que `VendorIconView` affiche son fallback (la lettre du vendor) au lieu de crasher l'app entière.

### Problème 2 — Plus de segments par défaut + CTA menu bar

- `MenuBarSegmentsSeeder.defaultSegments()` retourne `[]`. Le seeder reste appelé pour positionner `menuBarSegmentsInitialized = true` (idempotence préservée).
- Dans `MenuBarLabelRenderer.render(...)`, quand `segments.isEmpty`, utiliser le texte « Configure AI Metrics » (avec une icône `gearshape` ou similaire en préfixe pour le rendre repérable) au lieu de `"--"`.
- Le popover existant (`UsageDetailsView`) s'ouvre déjà au clic via `togglePopover`. Vérifier qu'il a un état vide cohérent qui guide vers Settings ; sinon ajouter un bouton « Open Settings » bien en évidence.
- Une fois qu'au moins un segment est configuré, le rendu reprend la logique actuelle.

### Release

Une fois les deux fixes mergés, taguer `v0.1.1` et laisser la CI release publier le nouveau cask. Cela permet aux utilisateurs Homebrew existants de faire `brew upgrade` et d'obtenir un binaire qui ne crashe plus, même si pour une raison X le bundle ressources manque.

---

## Fichiers à modifier

| Fichier | Lignes | Modification |
|---|---|---|
| `AIUsagesTrackers/Sources/App/VendorBranding.swift` | 105-116 | Remplacer `Bundle.module.url(...)` par un lookup multi-emplacements avec retour `nil` safe. Ne plus référencer `Bundle.module` directement. |
| `AIUsagesTrackers/Sources/AIUsagesTrackers/Preferences/MenuBarSegmentsSeeder.swift` | 9-24 | `defaultSegments()` retourne `[]`. |
| `AIUsagesTrackers/Sources/App/MenuBarLabelRenderer.swift` | 17-40 (et helpers) | Quand `segments.isEmpty` ET `fallbackText` indique l'absence de données utiles, rendre le label « Configure AI Metrics ». Préférable : ajouter un paramètre explicite `isUnconfigured: Bool` calculé dans `AppDelegate.refreshStatusItemImage()` à partir de `store.menuBarSegments.isEmpty`. |
| `AIUsagesTrackers/Sources/App/AppDelegate.swift` | 173-183 | Passer le flag `isUnconfigured` au renderer. |
| `AIUsagesTrackers/Sources/App/Views/UsageDetailsView.swift` (à confirmer) | empty state | S'assurer que l'utilisateur sans segment voit dans le popover un CTA explicite « Open Settings to add your first segment ». |
| `AIUsagesTrackers/Tests/...MenuBarSegmentsSeederTests.swift` | – | Mettre à jour le test qui asserte `defaultSegments().count == 2` (s'il existe) pour refléter le nouveau comportement (`isEmpty`). |
| `AIUsagesTrackers/Tests/...VendorBrandingTests.swift` (à créer si absent) | – | Test : `loadTemplateImage` retourne `nil` proprement quand le bundle de resources est absent (au lieu de crasher). |

## Réutilisation existante

- Le state vide UI dans Settings (`MenubarHintSettingsView.swift:115-133`) est déjà bien fait — on peut s'en inspirer pour l'état vide du popover s'il manque.
- Le script `scripts/build-app-bundle.sh:49-83` copie déjà les `.bundle` dans `Contents/MacOS/` ; le fix défensif est en plus, pas en remplacement.
- L'observabilité dark/light mode (`refreshStatusItemImage`) reste inchangée.

## Vérification

1. **Build local** : `swift build` puis `./scripts/build-app-bundle.sh` → lancer le `.app` produit.
2. **Test crash defensiveness** : supprimer manuellement `Contents/MacOS/AIUsagesTrackers_AIUsagesTrackers.bundle` du `.app` et relancer → l'app doit démarrer, la barre de menu doit afficher « Configure AI Metrics » avec un fallback texte pour les icônes vendor (pas de crash).
3. **Test onboarding** : effacer les UserDefaults (`defaults delete io.github.fcamblor.ai-usages-tracker`), relancer → la barre de menu affiche « Configure AI Metrics », aucun segment n'est seedé. Cliquer ouvre le popover ou les settings, ajouter un segment → le label se met à jour.
4. **Tests Swift** : `swift test` doit passer (tests seeder + nouveau test VendorBranding).
5. **CI release** : tag `v0.1.1` → vérifier que le nouveau zip publie bien le `.app` avec `Contents/MacOS/*.bundle` présent.

# Markdown authoring rules

## Language: English only

All Markdown files in this repository MUST be written in English, regardless of the conversation language. This includes `CLAUDE.md`, every file under `docs/`, `README.md`, and any new `*.md`.

- When creating a new Markdown file, write it in English from the start even if the user's prompt is in another language.
- When editing an existing Markdown file that contains non-English prose, translate the edited sections to English instead of preserving mixed languages.
- Code blocks, file paths, commands, and identifiers keep their original form — this rule applies to natural-language prose only.

## Write for longevity

Markdown in this repository is long-lived context. Optimize each file so it does not need to be rewritten as the code evolves. Avoid anything that ties documentation to a specific moment in time or to renamable artifacts:

- Do NOT reference specific commit SHAs, PR numbers, or branch names.
- Do NOT describe the project as being in a transient state ("currently a Hello World", "not yet implemented", "WIP"). If a feature does not exist, simply do not document it.
- Do NOT speculate about intent or future direction ("the name suggests…", "will eventually…"). Document what the code actually does today in terms that remain true tomorrow.
- Do NOT list every file, symbol, or dependency when a structural description is enough — exhaustive lists drift out of date quickly.
- Avoid naming specific files, types, or symbols that could be renamed or moved. Prefer describing roles and responsibilities ("the SwiftUI entry point", "the package manifest") over fixed identifiers. When a path is truly stable because a tool mandates it (e.g. `Package.swift` for Swift Package Manager, `.claude/rules/` for Claude Code), it is fine to use it.
- Do NOT duplicate information that already lives authoritatively in code or tool manifests (version numbers, platform minimums, target lists). Point readers at the source of truth instead of mirroring it.

When in doubt, ask: "Will this sentence still be accurate after the next three features ship, or after a plausible rename?" If not, rephrase or remove it.

## Single source of truth across Markdown files

When two Markdown files cover the same topic, exactly one is the authoritative spec; the other references it and adds only what is genuinely its own (workflow steps, scaffolding procedure, agent phases, etc.).

This applies in particular to these pairs:

- `docs/<topic>.md` (spec) ↔ `.claude/skills/<name>/SKILL.md` (skill that implements it)
- `docs/<topic>.md` (spec) ↔ `.claude/agents/<name>.md` (agent prompt that uses it)
- `docs/<topic>.md` (spec) ↔ `.claude/commands/<name>.md` (slash command that triggers it)

Rules:

- Templates, file formats, naming conventions, and directory layouts belong in the spec, NOT in the operational artifact.
- The operational artifact MUST start with a one-line pointer ("Read `docs/<topic>.md` first; it defines X, Y, Z.") and MUST include a conflict-resolution line ("If this artifact disagrees with `docs/<topic>.md`, the spec wins.").
- Before adding concrete content (a template block, a format string, a list of sections) to an operational artifact, check whether that content already exists in a `docs/` spec. If it does, reference it instead of copying it.

## Exception: `roadmap/`

Files under `roadmap/` intentionally describe planned, not-yet-implemented work. The "Write for longevity" rules above (no transient state, no speculation about future direction) do NOT apply there — a roadmap is precisely a record of intent and ordering that will evolve as features ship. The English-only rule still applies. See `docs/ROADMAP.md` for the roadmap file conventions.

36	        guard let tintHex = brand(for: vendor)?.tintHex else { return nil }
37	        return Color(hex: tintHex)
38	    }
39	
40	    static func icon(for vendor: Vendor) -> NSImage? {
41	        guard let assetName = brand(for: vendor)?.assetName else { return nil }
42	        return loadTemplateImage(named: assetName)
43	    }
44	
45	    /// Pixels of white halo added on each side around the vendor icon for legibility.
46	    static let iconGlowPadding: CGFloat = 3.0
47	
48	    /// Returns a tinted, non-template copy of the vendor icon at the requested height.
49	    /// Uses the brand's `tintHex` as fill color and the template PDF as an alpha mask.
50	    /// A crisp white halo is produced by drawing a white silhouette at pixel offsets
51	    /// in all directions — no blur, so the icon stays sharp on any menu bar tint color.
52	    @MainActor
53	    static func tintedNSImage(for vendor: Vendor, height: CGFloat) -> NSImage? {
54	        guard let template = icon(for: vendor),
55	              let brand = brand(for: vendor),
56	              let tintColor = NSColor(hex: brand.tintHex) else { return nil }
57	        let aspectRatio = template.size.height > 0
58	            ? template.size.width / template.size.height : 1
59	        let iconSize = NSSize(width: ceil(height * aspectRatio), height: ceil(height))
60	        let pad = Self.iconGlowPadding
61	        let canvasSize = NSSize(width: iconSize.width + pad * 2, height: iconSize.height + pad * 2)
62	        let iconRect = NSRect(x: pad, y: pad, width: iconSize.width, height: iconSize.height)
63	
64	        let tinted = NSImage(size: iconSize)
65	        tinted.lockFocus()
66	        tintColor.setFill()
67	        NSRect(origin: .zero, size: iconSize).fill()
68	        template.draw(
69	            in: NSRect(origin: .zero, size: iconSize),
70	            from: .zero,
71	            operation: .destinationIn,
72	            fraction: 1.0
73	        )
74	        tinted.unlockFocus()
75	
76	        let white = NSImage(size: iconSize)
77	        white.lockFocus()
78	        NSColor.white.setFill()
79	        NSRect(origin: .zero, size: iconSize).fill()
80	        template.draw(
81	            in: NSRect(origin: .zero, size: iconSize),
82	            from: .zero,
83	            operation: .destinationIn,
84	            fraction: 1.0
85	        )
86	        white.unlockFocus()
87	
88	        let result = NSImage(size: canvasSize)
89	        result.lockFocus()
90	        // Draw the white silhouette at every surrounding pixel offset to build a crisp halo.
91	        for dx: CGFloat in [-2, -1, 0, 1, 2] {
92	            for dy: CGFloat in [-2, -1, 0, 1, 2] where !(dx == 0 && dy == 0) {
93	                white.draw(in: NSRect(
94	                    x: iconRect.minX + dx, y: iconRect.minY + dy,
95	                    width: iconRect.width, height: iconRect.height
96	                ))
97	            }
98	        }
99	        tinted.draw(in: iconRect)
100	        result.unlockFocus()
101	        result.isTemplate = false
102	        return result
103	    }
104	
105	    private static func loadTemplateImage(named assetName: String) -> NSImage? {
106	        guard let url = Bundle.module.url(
107	            forResource: assetName,
108	            withExtension: "pdf",
109	            subdirectory: "VendorBranding"
110	        ), let image = NSImage(contentsOf: url) else {
111	            return nil
112	        }
113	
114	        image.isTemplate = true
115	        return image
116	    }
117	}

4	/// Rasterises the full menu bar label into a non-template `NSImage`.
5	/// MenuBarExtra's label was unusable: template tinting stripped the per-tier
6	/// dot colors, and mixed HStacks silently truncated segments past the first.
7	/// With a custom `NSStatusItem`, we control `isTemplate = false` so hues
8	/// survive and we pick the text color from the status button's effective
9	/// appearance (which tracks the menu bar, not the app).
10	@MainActor
11	enum MenuBarLabelRenderer {
12	    private static let font: NSFont = .menuBarFont(ofSize: 0)
13	    private static let dot = "● "
14	    private static let horizontalPadding: CGFloat = 4
15	    private static let height: CGFloat = 18
16	
17	    static func render(
18	        segments: [MenuBarSegment],
19	        separator: String,
20	        fallbackText: String,
21	        isDarkMenuBar: Bool
22	    ) -> NSImage {
23	        let textColor: NSColor = isDarkMenuBar ? .white : .black
24	        let attributed = attributedString(
25	            segments: segments,
26	            separator: separator,
27	            fallbackText: fallbackText,
28	            textColor: textColor
29	        )
30	        let textSize = attributed.size()
31	        let width = ceil(textSize.width) + horizontalPadding * 2
32	        let size = NSSize(width: max(width, 1), height: height)
33	        let image = NSImage(size: size)
34	        image.lockFocus()
35	        let textY = (height - ceil(textSize.height)) / 2
36	        attributed.draw(at: NSPoint(x: horizontalPadding, y: textY))
37	        image.unlockFocus()
38	        image.isTemplate = false
39	        return image
40	    }
41	
42	    private static func attributedString(
43	        segments: [MenuBarSegment],
44	        separator: String,
45	        fallbackText: String,
46	        textColor: NSColor
47	    ) -> NSAttributedString {
48	        guard !segments.isEmpty else {
49	            return NSAttributedString(
50	                string: fallbackText,
51	                attributes: [.font: font, .foregroundColor: textColor]
52	            )
53	        }
54	        let result = NSMutableAttributedString()
55	        for (index, segment) in segments.enumerated() {
56	            if index > 0, !separator.isEmpty {
57	                result.append(NSAttributedString(
58	                    string: separator,
59	                    attributes: [.font: font, .foregroundColor: textColor]
60	                ))
61	            }
62	            if let vendor = segment.vendorIcon,
63	               let icon = VendorBranding.tintedNSImage(for: vendor, height: font.capHeight) {
64	                let attachment = NSTextAttachment()
65	                attachment.image = icon
66	                let pad = VendorBranding.iconGlowPadding
67	                attachment.bounds = CGRect(
68	                    x: -pad,
69	                    y: -pad,
70	                    width: icon.size.width,
71	                    height: icon.size.height
72	                )
73	                result.append(NSAttributedString(attachment: attachment))
74	                result.append(NSAttributedString(
75	                    string: "\u{2009}",
76	                    attributes: [.font: font, .foregroundColor: textColor]
77	                ))
78	            }
79	            if segment.showDot {
80	                let dotColor = segment.tier?.nsColor ?? textColor
81	                result.append(NSAttributedString(
82	                    string: dot,
83	                    attributes: [.font: font, .foregroundColor: dotColor]
84	                ))
85	            }
86	            result.append(NSAttributedString(
87	                string: segment.text,
88	                attributes: [.font: font, .foregroundColor: textColor]
89	            ))
90	        }
91	        return result
92	    }
93	}

7	final class AppDelegate: NSObject, NSApplicationDelegate {
8	    private var poller: UsagePoller?

33	        // static icon slot — set the Dock / Cmd+Tab icon programmatically.

71	        let refreshState = RefreshState()
72	        let codexConnector = CodexConnector()

74	            connectors: [ClaudeCodeConnector(), codexConnector],
75	            statusConnectors: [ClaudeStatusConnector(), CodexStatusConnector()],
76	            fileManager: fileManager,
77	            refreshState: refreshState,
78	            preferences: Self.sharedPreferences
79	        )
80	        let accountMonitor = ClaudeActiveAccountMonitor(
81	            fileManager: fileManager,
82	            onActiveAccountChanged: { [weak poller] _ in
83	                await poller?.pollOnce(force: true)
84	            }
85	        )

87	            onActiveAccountChanged: { [weak codexConnector, weak poller] _ in
88	                await codexConnector?.invalidateEmailCache()

126	    // MARK: - Status item
127	
128	    private func setupStatusItem(
129	        store: UsageStore,
130	        refreshState: RefreshState,
131	        historyReader: UsageHistoryReader
132	    ) {
133	        let item = NSStatusBar.system.statusItem(withLength: NSStatusItem.variableLength)
134	        statusItem = item
135	
136	        let popover = NSPopover()
137	        popover.behavior = .transient
138	        let hosting = NSHostingController(
139	            rootView: UsageDetailsView(
140	                store: store,
141	                refreshState: refreshState,
142	                historyReader: historyReader,
143	                onRefresh: { [weak self] in
144	                    await self?.poller?.pollOnce(now: Date(), force: true)
145	                },
146	                onOpenSettings: { [weak self] in
147	                    self?.openSettings()
148	                },
149	                onQuit: { [weak self] in
150	                    self?.quit()
151	                }
152	            )
153	        )
154	        // Let SwiftUI's intrinsic size drive the popover so it grows with content
155	        // up to the screen-ratio cap enforced inside UsageDetailsView.
156	        hosting.sizingOptions = [.preferredContentSize]
157	        popover.contentViewController = hosting
158	        self.popover = popover
159	
160	        if let button = item.button {
161	            button.target = self
162	            button.action = #selector(togglePopover(_:))
163	            // The menu bar appearance can change (wallpaper-driven tinting) without
164	            // the app's effectiveAppearance changing, so we observe the button itself.
165	            appearanceObserver = button.observe(\.effectiveAppearance, options: [.new]) { [weak self] _, _ in
166	                Task { @MainActor in self?.refreshStatusItemImage() }
167	            }
168	        }
169	
170	        refreshStatusItemImage()
171	    }

173	    private func refreshStatusItemImage() {
174	        guard let store = usageStore, let button = statusItem?.button else { return }
175	        let isDark = button.effectiveAppearance.bestMatch(from: [.darkAqua, .aqua]) == .darkAqua
176	        let image = MenuBarLabelRenderer.render(
177	            segments: store.menuBarSegments,
178	            separator: Self.sharedPreferences.menuBarSeparator,
179	            fallbackText: store.menuBarText,
180	            isDarkMenuBar: isDark
181	        )
182	        button.image = image
183	    }

185	    @objc private func togglePopover(_ sender: Any?) {
186	        guard let popover, let button = statusItem?.button else { return }
187	        if popover.isShown {
188	            popover.performClose(sender)
189	        } else {
190	            popover.show(relativeTo: button.bounds, of: button, preferredEdge: .minY)
191	            popover.contentViewController?.view.window?.makeKey()
192	        }
193	    }

195	    // MARK: - Observation
196	
197	    /// Re-arms `withObservationTracking` after every change so the status item
198	    /// keeps mirroring the store. Tracking fires exactly once per registration.
199	    private func trackStoreChanges(store: UsageStore) {
200	        withObservationTracking {
201	            _ = store.menuBarSegments
202	            _ = store.menuBarText
203	        } onChange: { [weak self] in
204	            Task { @MainActor in
205	                guard let self, let store = self.usageStore else { return }
206	                self.refreshStatusItemImage()
207	                self.trackStoreChanges(store: store)
208	            }
209	        }
210	    }

212	    // MARK: - Settings
213	
214	    private func openSettings() {
215	        popover?.performClose(nil)
216	        // .accessory apps have no dock presence; showSettingsWindow: silently fails
217	        // unless we temporarily switch to .regular so AppKit can make the window key.
218	        NSApp.setActivationPolicy(.regular)
219	        NSApp.activate(ignoringOtherApps: true)
220	
221	        // SwiftUI creates the Settings window lazily on first showSettingsWindow: —
222	        // if it already exists, reuse it directly to avoid responder-chain races
223	        // that occur during the popover-dismissal + activation-policy transition.
224	        if let settingsWindow = Self.findSettingsWindow() {
225	            settingsWindow.makeKeyAndOrderFront(nil)
226	        } else {
227	            // First-time path: AppKit needs a runloop tick after the .accessory →
228	            // .regular transition to install the standard App menu (which is where
229	            // SwiftUI wires the Settings… action). sendAction with a nil target
230	            // relies on that wiring, so we trigger the menu item directly once
231	            // AppKit has had a chance to build it.
232	            DispatchQueue.main.asyncAfter(deadline: .now() + 0.15) {
233	                Self.triggerSettingsMenuItem()
234	                if let settingsWindow = Self.findSettingsWindow() {
235	                    settingsWindow.makeKeyAndOrderFront(nil)
236	                }
237	            }
238	        }
239	
240	        // Restore .accessory policy once the settings window is closed.
241	        if settingsWindowObserver == nil {
242	            settingsWindowObserver = NotificationCenter.default.addObserver(
243	                forName: NSWindow.willCloseNotification,
244	                object: nil,
245	                queue: .main
246	            ) { [weak self] notification in
247	                guard let self else { return }
248	                // Only react to the Settings window closing — popovers and other
249	                // transient windows also emit willClose.
250	                guard let closingWindow = notification.object as? NSWindow else { return }
251	                let windowIdentifier = closingWindow.identifier?.rawValue ?? ""
252	                guard windowIdentifier.contains("Settings") || windowIdentifier.contains("Preferences") else { return }
253	                // willClose fires while the window is still visible and before
254	                // AppKit tears it down; defer to the next runloop tick so the
255	                // activation-policy change isn't overridden by pending AppKit work.
256	                DispatchQueue.main.async {
257	                    NSApp.setActivationPolicy(.accessory)
258	                    // Accessory transition doesn't always remove the app from the
259	                    // Cmd+Tab / app-switcher list until another app is activated.
260	                    if let frontmost = NSWorkspace.shared.runningApplications.first(where: {
261	                        $0.isActive && $0.processIdentifier != ProcessInfo.processInfo.processIdentifier
262	                    }) {
263	                        frontmost.activate()
264	                    } else {
265	                        NSApp.hide(nil)
266	                    }
267	                    if let token = self.settingsWindowObserver {
268	                        NotificationCenter.default.removeObserver(token)
269	                        self.settingsWindowObserver = nil
270	                    }
271	                }
272	            }
273	        }

276	    /// SwiftUI tags its Settings window with an identifier containing "Settings"
277	    /// (or "Preferences" on older SDKs). Match on the stable identifier rather
278	    /// than localized titles.
279	    private static func findSettingsWindow() -> NSWindow? {
280	        NSApp.windows.first { window in
281	            let identifier = window.identifier?.rawValue ?? ""
282	            return identifier.contains("Settings") || identifier.contains("Preferences")
283	        }
284	    }

286	    /// Walk the main menu looking for the App menu's Settings… (or Preferences…)
287	    /// item and invoke it. Going through the menu guarantees the action is
288	    /// routed to whichever responder SwiftUI wired up, instead of relying on
289	    /// `sendAction(_:to:from:)` to resolve a nil target in the responder chain
290	    /// — which is unreliable right after an activation-policy switch.
291	    private static func triggerSettingsMenuItem() {
292	        guard let mainMenu = NSApp.mainMenu else { return }
293	        for topItem in mainMenu.items {
294	            guard let submenu = topItem.submenu else { continue }
295	            for (index, item) in submenu.items.enumerated() {
296	                let title = item.title
297	                if title.hasPrefix("Settings") || title.hasPrefix("Preferences") {
298	                    submenu.performActionForItem(at: index)
299	                    return
300	                }
301	            }
302	        }
303	    }

305	    // MARK: - Quit

307	    private func quit() {

309	        let schedulerRef = snapshotScheduler
310	        let monitorRef = accountMonitor
311	        let codexMonitorRef = codexMonitor
312	        Task {
313	            await pollerRef?.stop()
314	            await schedulerRef?.stop()
315	            await monitorRef?.stop()
316	            await codexMonitorRef?.stop()
317	        }
318	        usageStore?.stop()
319	        pidGuard?.release()
320	        NSApplication.shared.terminate(nil)

#!/usr/bin/env bash
#
# Build a distributable .app bundle for the menubar app.
# Output: dist/AI Usages Tracker.app (ad-hoc signed, unsealed).
#
set -euo pipefail

SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
REPO_ROOT="$(cd "$SCRIPT_DIR/.." && pwd)"
PACKAGE_DIR="$REPO_ROOT/AIUsagesTrackers"
DIST_DIR="$REPO_ROOT/dist"

APP_DISPLAY_NAME="AI Usages Tracker"
APP_BINARY_NAME="AIUsagesTrackers"
BUNDLE_ID="io.github.fcamblor.ai-usages-tracker"
BUNDLE_VERSION="${BUNDLE_VERSION:-0.1.0}"
MIN_MACOS_VERSION="14.0"

APP_BUNDLE="$DIST_DIR/$APP_DISPLAY_NAME.app"
CONTENTS="$APP_BUNDLE/Contents"
MACOS_DIR="$CONTENTS/MacOS"
RESOURCES_DIR="$CONTENTS/Resources"
BUILD_CONFIG="release"
BUILD_DIR="$PACKAGE_DIR/.build/$BUILD_CONFIG"

echo "→ Building release binaries"
cd "$PACKAGE_DIR"
swift build -c "$BUILD_CONFIG" --product "$APP_BINARY_NAME"
swift build -c "$BUILD_CONFIG" --product IconExporter

# Resolve absolute build paths (swift emits a symlink in some configurations).
APP_BIN="$(swift build -c "$BUILD_CONFIG" --show-bin-path)/$APP_BINARY_NAME"
ICON_EXPORTER_BIN="$(swift build -c "$BUILD_CONFIG" --show-bin-path)/IconExporter"

echo "→ Assembling bundle at $APP_BUNDLE"
rm -rf "$APP_BUNDLE"
mkdir -p "$MACOS_DIR" "$RESOURCES_DIR"

echo "→ Rendering .icns from AppIconView"
ICONSET_TMP="$(mktemp -d)/AppIcon.iconset"
mkdir -p "$ICONSET_TMP"
"$ICON_EXPORTER_BIN" "$ICONSET_TMP"
iconutil -c icns "$ICONSET_TMP" -o "$RESOURCES_DIR/AppIcon.icns"

echo "→ Copying executable"
cp "$APP_BIN" "$MACOS_DIR/$APP_BINARY_NAME"
chmod +x "$MACOS_DIR/$APP_BINARY_NAME"

# SwiftPM emits a resource bundle next to the binary for any target declaring
# `resources:`. The generated `Bundle.module` accessor looks for it relative to
# the executable URL, so it must sit alongside the binary in Contents/MacOS —
# not in Contents/Resources. Without this, the app fatal-errors on first access.
echo "→ Copying SwiftPM resource bundles"
BIN_PATH="$(swift build -c "$BUILD_CONFIG" --show-bin-path)"
shopt -s nullglob
for bundle in "$BIN_PATH"/*.bundle; do
  bundle_name="$(basename "$bundle")"
  echo "  • $bundle_name"
  cp -R "$bundle" "$MACOS_DIR/"
  # SwiftPM emits a flat bundle (resources at the root, no Info.plist).
  # codesign rejects that as "bundle format unrecognized" — give it a minimal
  # Info.plist so the embedded bundle can be signed with the parent .app.
  bundle_basename="${bundle_name%.bundle}"
  cat > "$MACOS_DIR/$bundle_name/Info.plist" <<PLIST
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<!DOCTYPE plist PUBLIC "-//Apple//DTD PLIST 1.0//EN" "http://www.apple.com/DTDs/PropertyList-1.0.dtd">
<plist version="1.0">
<dict>
  <key>CFBundleIdentifier</key>
  <string>$BUNDLE_ID.resources.$bundle_basename</string>
  <key>CFBundleName</key>
  <string>$bundle_basename</string>
  <key>CFBundlePackageType</key>
  <string>BNDL</string>
  <key>CFBundleShortVersionString</key>
  <string>$BUNDLE_VERSION</string>
  <key>CFBundleVersion</key>
  <string>$BUNDLE_VERSION</string>
</dict>
</plist>
PLIST
done
shopt -u nullglob

echo "→ Writing Info.plist"
cat > "$CONTENTS/Info.plist" <<PLIST
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<!DOCTYPE plist PUBLIC "-//Apple//DTD PLIST 1.0//EN" "http://www.apple.com/DTDs/PropertyList-1.0.dtd">
<plist version="1.0">
<dict>
  <key>CFBundleName</key>
  <string>$APP_DISPLAY_NAME</string>
  <key>CFBundleDisplayName</key>
  <string>$APP_DISPLAY_NAME</string>
  <key>CFBundleExecutable</key>
  <string>$APP_BINARY_NAME</string>
  <key>CFBundleIdentifier</key>
  <string>$BUNDLE_ID</string>
  <key>CFBundlePackageType</key>
  <string>APPL</string>
  <key>CFBundleShortVersionString</key>
  <string>$BUNDLE_VERSION</string>
  <key>CFBundleVersion</key>
  <string>$BUNDLE_VERSION</string>
  <key>CFBundleIconFile</key>
  <string>AppIcon</string>
  <key>LSMinimumSystemVersion</key>
  <string>$MIN_MACOS_VERSION</string>
  <key>LSUIElement</key>
  <true/>
  <key>NSHighResolutionCapable</key>
  <true/>
  <key>NSHumanReadableCopyright</key>
  <string>© $(date +%Y) Frédéric Camblor</string>
</dict>
</plist>
PLIST

echo "→ Ad-hoc code-signing (required for Gatekeeper on unsigned binaries)"
codesign --force --deep --sign - "$APP_BUNDLE"

echo
echo "✓ Bundle ready: $APP_BUNDLE"
echo "  Launch with: open \"$APP_BUNDLE\""

23	
24	    /// Shared usage store — populated in applicationDidFinishLaunching. The Settings

29	    func applicationDidFinishLaunching(_ notification: Notification) {
30	        NSApplication.shared.setActivationPolicy(.accessory)
31	
32	        // No .app bundle ships with this SwiftPM executable, so AppKit has no
33	        // static icon slot — set the Dock / Cmd+Tab icon programmatically.
34	        if let icon = AppIconRenderer.makeImage(pixelSize: 1024) {
35	            NSApplication.shared.applicationIconImage = icon
36	        }
37	
38	        let home = FileManager.default.homeDirectoryForCurrentUser.path
39	        let guard_ = AppPidGuard(cacheDir: "\(home)/.cache/ai-usages-tracker")
40	        do {
41	            try guard_.acquire()
42	        } catch AppPidGuardError.alreadyRunning(let pid, _) {
43	            let alert = NSAlert()
44	            alert.messageText = "AI Usages Tracker is already running"
45	            alert.informativeText = "Another instance is active (PID \(pid)). Use the menu bar icon to quit it before launching a new one."
46	            alert.runModal()
47	            NSApplication.shared.terminate(nil)
48	            return
49	        } catch {
50	            let alert = NSAlert()
51	            alert.messageText = "Failed to start AI Usages Tracker"
52	            alert.informativeText = error.localizedDescription
53	            alert.runModal()

55	            return
56	        }
57	        pidGuard = guard_

59	        Loggers.setPreferences(Self.sharedPreferences)
60	
61	        MenuBarSegmentsSeeder.seedIfNeeded(preferences: Self.sharedPreferences)
62

4	/// Rasterises the full menu bar label into a non-template `NSImage`.
5	/// MenuBarExtra's label was unusable: template tinting stripped the per-tier
6	/// dot colors, and mixed HStacks silently truncated segments past the first.
7	/// With a custom `NSStatusItem`, we control `isTemplate = false` so hues
8	/// survive and we pick the text color from the status button's effective
9	/// appearance (which tracks the menu bar, not the app).
10	@MainActor
11	enum MenuBarLabelRenderer {
12	    private static let font: NSFont = .menuBarFont(ofSize: 0)
13	    private static let dot = "● "
14	    private static let horizontalPadding: CGFloat = 4
15	    private static let height: CGFloat = 18
16	
17	    static func render(
18	        segments: [MenuBarSegment],
19	        separator: String,
20	        fallbackText: String,
21	        isDarkMenuBar: Bool
22	    ) -> NSImage {
23	        let textColor: NSColor = isDarkMenuBar ? .white : .black
24	        let attributed = attributedString(
25	            segments: segments,
26	            separator: separator,
27	            fallbackText: fallbackText,
28	            textColor: textColor
29	        )
30	        let textSize = attributed.size()
31	        let width = ceil(textSize.width) + horizontalPadding * 2
32	        let size = NSSize(width: max(width, 1), height: height)
33	        let image = NSImage(size: size)
34	        image.lockFocus()
35	        let textY = (height - ceil(textSize.height)) / 2
36	        attributed.draw(at: NSPoint(x: horizontalPadding, y: textY))
37	        image.unlockFocus()
38	        image.isTemplate = false
39	        return image
40	    }
41	
42	    private static func attributedString(
43	        segments: [MenuBarSegment],
44	        separator: String,
45	        fallbackText: String,
46	        textColor: NSColor
47	    ) -> NSAttributedString {
48	        guard !segments.isEmpty else {
49	            return NSAttributedString(
50	                string: fallbackText,
51	                attributes: [.font: font, .foregroundColor: textColor]
52	            )
53	        }
54	        let result = NSMutableAttributedString()
55	        for (index, segment) in segments.enumerated() {
56	            if index > 0, !separator.isEmpty {
57	                result.append(NSAttributedString(
58	                    string: separator,
59	                    attributes: [.font: font, .foregroundColor: textColor]
60	                ))
61	            }
62	            if let vendor = segment.vendorIcon,
63	               let icon = VendorBranding.tintedNSImage(for: vendor, height: font.capHeight) {
64	                let attachment = NSTextAttachment()
65	                attachment.image = icon
66	                let pad = VendorBranding.iconGlowPadding
67	                attachment.bounds = CGRect(
68	                    x: -pad,
69	                    y: -pad,
70	                    width: icon.size.width,
71	                    height: icon.size.height
72	                )
73	                result.append(NSAttributedString(attachment: attachment))
74	                result.append(NSAttributedString(
75	                    string: "\u{2009}",
76	                    attributes: [.font: font, .foregroundColor: textColor]
77	                ))
78	            }
79	            if segment.showDot {
80	                let dotColor = segment.tier?.nsColor ?? textColor
81	                result.append(NSAttributedString(
82	                    string: dot,
83	                    attributes: [.font: font, .foregroundColor: dotColor]
84	                ))
85	            }
86	            result.append(NSAttributedString(
87	                string: segment.text,
88	                attributes: [.font: font, .foregroundColor: textColor]
89	            ))
90	        }
91	        return result
92	    }
93	}

Tout est implémenté, builds + tests verts.

**Récapitulatif des changements** :

1. **`VendorBranding.swift`** : helper `resourceBundle` qui probe plusieurs emplacements (`Bundle.main.resourceURL`, `Contents/MacOS/`, `Contents/Resources/`, etc.) sans jamais toucher `Bundle.module` (qui trap si l'asset manque). `loadTemplateImage` retourne `nil` proprement, et `VendorIconView` affiche désormais la première lettre du vendor en fallback.

2. **`MenuBarSegmentsSeeder.swift`** : `defaultSegments()` retourne `[]`, idempotence préservée via `menuBarSegmentsInitialized`.

3. **`UsageStore.swift`** : les défauts S+W (utilisés par les tests/callers sans préférences) sont inlinés dans `makeDefaultPreferences()`, découplés du seeder onboarding.

4. **`MenuBarLabelRenderer.swift`** : nouveau paramètre `isUnconfigured` ; quand activé et qu'aucun segment, rend l'icône `gearshape` + « Configure AI Metrics ».

5. **`AppDelegate.refreshStatusItemImage()`** : passe `isUnconfigured = preferences.menuBarSegments.isEmpty`.

6. **`UsageDetailsView.swift`** : nouvel état vide « No menu bar segment yet » avec bouton « Open Settings » quand l'utilisateur n'a pas encore configuré de segment.

7. **Tests** : `MenuBarSegmentsSeederTests` mis à jour (asserte `isEmpty`), nouveau test `VendorBranding.icon` retournant `nil` au lieu de trapper.

Vérifié : `swift test` (387/387) ✅, `./scripts/build-app-bundle.sh` ✅.

content.text:
Confirmé : tu as `menuBarSegmentsInitialized = 1` et un `menuBarSegments` non-vide (866 bytes). Le seeder ne re-tourne donc pas, et l'app affiche les segments déjà persistés.

Pour tester l'onboarding « from scratch » :

```bash
defaults delete AIUsagesTrackers ai-tracker.menuBarSegments
defaults delete AIUsagesTrackers ai-tracker.menuBarSegmentsInitialized
swift run AIUsagesTrackers
```

Ou plus radical (efface toutes les prefs de l'app dev) :

```bash
defaults delete AIUsagesTrackers
swift run AIUsagesTrackers
```

À ce moment-là, le seeder marquera `menuBarSegmentsInitialized=true` avec un tableau vide, et la barre de menu affichera l'icône engrenage + « Configure AI Metrics ». Le clic ouvrira le popover avec le CTA « Open Settings ».

Note : le domaine UserDefaults dépend du contexte de lancement :
- `swift run` → domaine `AIUsagesTrackers` (nom de l'exécutable)
- `.app` Homebrew → `io.github.fcamblor.ai-usages-tracker` (bundle ID du `Info.plist`)

Donc nettoie le bon en fonction de ce que tu testes.

content.text:
# Smart commit

Tu dois committer les changements git en cours dans le repo courant en suivant les guidelines ci-dessous.

## Contexte utilisateur

Intent fourni par l'utilisateur (peut être vide) : ``

## Étapes

### 1. Analyser l'état du repo

Lance en parallèle :
- `git status` (sans `-uall`)
- `git diff --staged` et `git diff` pour voir les changements
- `git log -n 5 --pretty=format:'%h %s%n%b%n---'` pour voir les 4-5 derniers commits

### 2. Détecter les conventions du projet

À partir des 4-5 derniers commits :
- **Conventional commits ?** Si la majorité suit le format `type(scope): message` (feat, fix, chore, docs, refactor, test, perf, build, ci...) → utilise ce format. Sinon → format libre.
- **Langue ?** Détecte la langue dominante des messages (français, anglais, autre) → utilise la même langue. En cas de doute, français.
- **Style ?** Impératif vs descriptif, majuscule initiale ou non, présence de scope, etc. → imite.

### 3. Détecter un éventuel fixup

Si l'intent utilisateur mentionne un correctif (mots-clés : "fix", "corrige", "répare", "bug", "issue", "problème", "régression", "broken"...) :

1. Identifie les fichiers modifiés (`git diff --name-only HEAD` + fichiers staged).
2. Pour chaque fichier, regarde `git log -n 10 --oneline -- <fichier>` pour voir quels commits récents l'ont touché.
3. Si **un seul commit récent** (dans les ~5 derniers, non encore pushé sur main/master partagé — vérifie avec `git log @{u}..HEAD` si upstream) est clairement à l'origine du code cassé, propose un **`git commit --fixup=<sha>`** au lieu d'un commit classique.
4. Si ambigu ou si le commit date d'avant `@{u}` (déjà pushé en amont partagé), reste sur un commit classique.

Ne fais **pas** de rebase autosquash automatiquement — laisse ce soin à l'utilisateur.

### 4. Rédiger le message

Règles impératives :
- **Le message explique le POURQUOI**, pas le QUOI. Le diff dit déjà ce qui change. Le message dit pourquoi ce changement existe (motivation, contrainte, problème résolu, contexte métier).
- Sujet court (≤72 caractères), en suivant la convention détectée.
- Si le pourquoi ne tient pas dans le sujet, ajoute un corps (ligne vide + explication). Sinon, pas de corps inutile.
- Pas de name-dropping de fichiers/fonctions dans le sujet — le diff suffit.
- Pas de phrases vides genre « Mise à jour du code » ou « Divers fixes ».

Si l'intent utilisateur est vide ou trop vague pour déduire le pourquoi, et que le diff ne le rend pas évident, **demande à l'utilisateur** avant de committer plutôt que d'inventer.

### 5. Staging et commit

- Si rien n'est staged, stage les fichiers pertinents **par nom** (jamais `git add -A` ni `git add .` pour éviter d'embarquer secrets/binaires).
- Ignore les fichiers suspects (`.env`, `credentials*`, clés privées, dumps volumineux). Préviens l'utilisateur si tu en vois.
- Crée le commit via HEREDOC pour préserver le formatage multiligne.
- **Ne jamais** utiliser `--no-verify`, `--amend` (sauf demande explicite), ni `--no-gpg-sign`.
- Si un hook pre-commit échoue : diagnostique la cause, corrige, re-stage, et crée un **nouveau** commit (pas d'amend).

### 6. Vérification finale

Après commit, lance `git status` et `git log -1` pour confirmer. Reporte brièvement : sha court + sujet + (fixup le cas échéant).

## Garde-fous

- Ne push jamais.
- Ne crée pas de commit vide.
- Ne touche pas à `git config`.
- Si plusieurs "unités logiques" distinctes sont mélangées dans le diff, préviens l'utilisateur et propose de splitter plutôt que de tout entasser. Tu peux utiliser `git stash` (avec `-u`/`--keep-index` si pertinent) pour isoler des sous-ensembles — mais cette commande n'est **pas** auto-approuvée, l'utilisateur devra confirmer à chaque usage : ne l'utilise que si c'est vraiment nécessaire.

ad3ad63 docs: limit screenshot heights to improve README layout
Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Entire-Checkpoint: a24b8e70365a

---
5c70139 fix(build-app-bundle): include SwiftPM resource bundles in app bundle
SwiftPM emits resource bundles (AIUsagesTrackers_AIUsagesTrackers.bundle)
next to the executable when targets declare `resources:`. The build script
was not copying these into the .app bundle, causing Bundle.module to
fatal-error on first access (crash before applicationDidFinishLaunching
completed, so seeding never ran).

Now copies all *.bundle files from the build directory alongside the binary
in Contents/MacOS/, where Bundle.module looks for them. Also generates a
minimal Info.plist in each bundle so codesign --deep accepts the flat bundle
format and can sign the parent app without rejecting embedded bundles.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 <noreply@anthropic.com>

5c70139 fix(build-app-bundle): include SwiftPM resource bundles in app bundle
SwiftPM emits resource bundles (AIUsagesTrackers_AIUsagesTrackers.bundle)
next to the executable when targets declare `resources:`. The build script
was not copying these into the .app bundle, causing Bundle.module to
fatal-error on first access (crash before applicationDidFinishLaunching
completed, so seeding never ran).

Now copies all *.bundle files from the build directory alongside the binary
in Contents/MacOS/, where Bundle.module looks for them. Also generates a
minimal Info.plist in each bundle so codesign --deep accepts the flat bundle
format and can sign the parent app without rejecting embedded bundles.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 <noreply@anthropic.com>
